Let me craft plan description:

```
Plan:
1. Create backup of /etc/fstab using dd: dd if=/etc/fstab of=/etc/fstab.bak
2. Generate updated fstab into /tmp/fstab.new using awk:
   awk 'BEGIN {FS=" "; OFS=" "} {
       if ($1 ~ /^#|^$/) {print; next}
       if ($1 != "/" && $2 ~ /^\/dev\//) {
           if ($4 !~ /nodev/) {
               if ($4 != "") $4 = $4",nodev"; else $4="nodev"
           }
       }
       print
   }' /etc/fstab > /tmp/fstab.new
3. Replace original fstab: mv /tmp/fstab.new /etc/fstab
4. Validate the new fstab: mount -a
5. If mount -a fails, restore backup: mv /etc/fstab.bak /etc/fstab
```

Also mention that we didn't duplicate lines.
